name: ExtensionSubstanceDefinition_SourceMaterial_Type
title: R5: Classification of the origin of the raw material. e.g. cat hair is an Animal source type (new)
purpose:
This extension is part of the cross-version definitions generated to enable use of the
element `SubstanceDefinition.sourceMaterial.type` as defined in FHIR R5
in FHIR R4.

The source element is defined as:
`SubstanceDefinition.sourceMaterial.type` 0..1 `CodeableConcept`

Across FHIR versions, the element set has been mapped as:
*  R5: `SubstanceDefinition.sourceMaterial.type` 0..1 `CodeableConcept`
*  R4B: `SubstanceDefinition.sourceMaterial.type` 0..1 `CodeableConcept`

Following are the generation technical comments:
Element `SubstanceDefinition.sourceMaterial.type` has a context of SubstanceSpecification.sourceMaterial based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `SubstanceSpecification`.
Element `SubstanceDefinition.sourceMaterial.type` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.short: R5: Classification of the origin of the raw material. e.g. cat hair is an Animal source type (new)
element.comment:
Element `SubstanceDefinition.sourceMaterial.type` has a context of SubstanceSpecification.sourceMaterial based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `SubstanceSpecification`.
Element `SubstanceDefinition.sourceMaterial.type` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.short: Classification of the origin of the raw material. e.g. cat hair is an Animal source type
element.definition: A classification that provides the origin of the raw material. Example: cat hair would be an Animal source type.

binding.description: A classification that provides the origin of the substance raw material.
